original_abstract:
While artificial intelligence has long been a fixture of the technological landscape, the field has recently experienced an unprecedented surge in public attention, driven by rapid innovations from industry leaders such as Google, Microsoft, and Meta. Central to this shift is OpenAI’s release of ChatGPT, a landmark development in generative AI. Built on the Transformer architecture, this Large Language Model (LLM) leverages deep learning algorithms to produce remarkably nuanced, human-like responses within conversational contexts. Its massive parameter scale and open-domain training render it a versatile tool across diverse sectors, including customer service, automated content creation, and real-time translation—effectively signaling a paradigm shift in human-computer interaction.

However, the rapid integration of ChatGPT into daily life has also introduced significant ethical, social, and economic complexities that necessitate rigorous evaluation. This paper explores the technical foundations of ChatGPT, examining its underlying architecture and training methodologies while highlighting its broader societal implications. We propose a multi-faceted framework—integrating technological safeguards, regulatory policy, academic reform, and ethical guidelines—designed to harness the benefits of this technology while mitigating its potential risks. Ultimately, this study aims to foster a more comprehensive understanding of LLMs and provide a roadmap for their responsible evolution in a changing world.
humanized_abstract:
Despite being part of our technology repertoire for some time now, AI has recently shot into the limelight thanks to numerous breakthroughs announced by tech giants like Google, Microsoft and Meta. OpenAI recently revolutionized generative AI with the launch of ChatGPT — a Large Language Model (LLM) based on the Transformer architecture, utilizing deep learning techniques to facilitate highly human and natural interactions during conversations. Owing to its large number of parameters and open-domain training, this LLM can be used across a variety of applications such as customer support, content generation and real-time language translation — therefore revolutionizing the way humans interact with technology.

While ChatGPT is increasingly being used in many contexts, many ethical, social and economic challenges arising from its mass adoption require more in-depth research and analysis. In this study, we examine the technology that underpins the workings of ChatGPT by looking at the design architecture and training process of the Language Model behind the Chat interface, while highlighting at the same time its associated socio-economic implications. Our work is anchored within an all-encompassing safe-guarding model which harmonises technological safeguards, regulatory actions, academia innovation as well as ethics standards pertaining to the impact of such an innovative technology. This work aims therefore to help create a deeper knowledge around Large Language Models and, generally, contribute to their meaningful development that fits to ever-changing global needs and requirements.
